title: hieracium atratum subsp. polycephalum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. polycephalum, also known as the spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its multiple flower heads and dark, spotted leaves. The spotted hawkweed has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Hieracium atratum subsp. polycephalum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various applications in herbal medicine.
